Name: name_dom
Description: The base domain name string used as a prefix for output filenames.

Name: FE_ref_GIS_nc
Description: Full path to the GeoSpec output netCDF file produced by GeoSpec.py. |br| This is a required upstream dependency.

Name: FE_params_file
Description: Full path to the FastEddy parameters file (.in) containing grid dimensions and resolution.

Name: center_lat
Description: Latitude (decimal degrees) of the desired simulation domain center. |br| Used to locate the nearest point in the GIS dataset and anchor the simulation grid.

Name: center_lon
Description: Longitude (decimal degrees) of the desired simulation domain center. |br| Used with ‘center_lat’ to define grid placement.

Name: urban_opt
Description: Enables urban building processing. |br| If 0: building fields are not computed or written. |br| If 1: reads BuildingHeights from the GeoSpec netCDF, constructs a 3-D building mask array (BuildingMask), and writes both to the output. Must match the value set in GeoSpec.

Name: FE_new_nc_path
Description: Output directory for the SimGrid netCDF, binary terrain file (name_dom_Topography_NxN.dat), and optional diagnostic plot. |br| Created automatically if it does not exist.

Name: name_dom_add
Description: Optional suffix appended to ‘name_dom’ in output filenames. |br| Can be empty.

Name: urban_heatRedis_opt
Description: Enables urban surface heat flux redistribution. Only evaluated when urban_opt=1. |br| If 0: heat redistribution field is not computed. |br| If 1: reads z0 lookup tables from ‘landcover_table’ and computes a 2-D UrbanHeatRedis field written to the output netCDF file.

Name: landcover_table
Description: Full path to the land-cover CSV lookup table. Only read when urban_opt=1 and urban_heatRedis_opt=1. |br| Used to determine original and modified z0 values for heat flux redistribution over urban areas. Requires a fourth column with the modified z0 corresponding to surface vegetation only (excluding the building contribution).

Name: topo_average_opt
Description: Controls how terrain elevation is resampled from the GIS resolution to the simulation grid resolution. |br| If 0: direct sampling or interpolation. Nearest-neighbor decimation when the simulation grid is an integer multiple of GIS resolution, spline interpolation otherwise. |br| If 1: block averaging smooths sub-grid terrain variability (prevents aliasing).

Name: save_plot_opt
Description: Controls diagnostic plot generation. |br| If 0: no plot is created. |br| If 1: saves a 2-panel PNG showing vertical grid level profiles at the lowest- and highest-elevation points in the domain.
